ogTitle: Auchenflower vs Augustine Heights: Climate Safety
ogDescription: Data-driven comparison of climate risk in Auchenflower and Augustine Heights.
heading: Auchenflower vs Augustine Heights — Which is Safer?
intro: Compare climate risk between Auchenflower, Queensland and Augustine Heights, Queensland. Our analysis uses data from BOM, CSIRO, and state hazard authorities to score each suburb across 6 climate hazards.
verdict: Auchenflower has a lower overall climate risk score (32/100) compared to Augustine Heights (36/100). We recommend getting an address-level report for properties in both suburbs before making a purchase decision.

faq.question: Is Auchenflower or Augustine Heights safer from flooding?
faq.answer: Auchenflower has a flood risk score of 52/100, while Augustine Heights scores 53/100. A lower score indicates lower flood risk. Get an address-level flood risk report for a specific property.

faq.question: Which suburb has lower bushfire risk?
faq.answer: Auchenflower scores 23/100 for bushfire risk. Augustine Heights scores 22/100. Fire risk depends on proximity to vegetation and local fire history.

faq.question: Should I buy in Auchenflower or Augustine Heights?
faq.answer: Both suburbs have climate considerations. Auchenflower has a lower overall climate risk score. We recommend getting a full ClimateNest report for any specific property you're considering, which includes 8 hazard scores, insurance projections, and actionable recommendations.

valueImpact.description: Properties in this area may face a 0.92% climate value drag over time due to rising insurance costs and climate adaptation expenses.
